description_long: This object is a detailed model of a NASA Space Shuttle orbiter, featuring a streamlined white fuselage with black thermal protection tiles along the nose, wing edges, and underbelly. The wings are delta-shaped and low-mounted, with a slight upward dihedral and black leading edges. At the rear, three large main engines are flanked by two smaller orbital maneuvering system pods, and a vertical stabilizer rises above the tail. The cockpit is located at the forward end, with a distinctive black windshield and several small windows. The landing gear is visible beneath the fuselage, with two main gear units and a single nose gear. The shuttle's surface is smooth and hard, with visible panel lines and markings typical of space vehicles.
description: A white NASA Space Shuttle orbiter with black accents, delta wings, and three large rear engines. Features a forward cockpit, detailed surface markings, and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full delta wing shape, black leading edges, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the wing profile, and the rear engine cluster.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three main engines, two maneuvering pods, and the vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite wing and the shuttle's overall profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing delta configuration with slight upward dihedral and black leading edges; no winglets present.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines with two additional orbital maneuvering system pods.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and integrated horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a black windshield and multiple windows.
